title: PROMETHEUS Forge: Live ServerForge Case Study

welcome-to-the-forge:
# WELCOME TO THE PROMETHEUS FORGE

This server is the observable case-study surface for **PROMETHEUS V-1.1.1**, a candidate-to-proof software engineering runtime. PROMETHEUS accepts a mission, creates competing implementations in isolated Git worktrees, runs standard tests, challenges the leader adversarially, preserves repair history, promotes one result, and seals the evidence into receipts and capability memory.

**Read the server in this order**
1. `#live-case-study` — competition narrative and run milestones.
2. `#proof-index` — public evidence and reproduction links.
3. Mission Control — intake, candidate activity, and promotion decisions.
4. Adversarial Twin — findings, attacks, repairs, and retests.
5. ProofGrid — receipts, capability genomes, and reproduction.
6. ServerForge Ops — bridge health, telemetry, incidents, and recovery.

Discord is the observability layer. EDEN is the local execution body. PROMETHEUS governs the command-to-proof lifecycle.
live-case-study:
# 🜏 GHOST ATLAS INSTITUTE FIELD REPORT
**PROMETHEUS Forge: Live ServerForge Case Study**

**Report:** `GAI-FR-20260721-PROM-SF-001`
**Classification:** Public Competition Field Report
**Evidence state:** Screenshot-grounded · Repository-verified · Live-node-verified

This report documents one inspectable engineering lineage:
```text
EDEN local execution body
→ Codex implementation and hardening
→ PROMETHEUS Command-to-Proof governance
→ ServerForge observability and publication
→ GitHub repository and public proof node
```
The report establishes observable behavior and evidence lineage without presenting Discord as the execution runtime or exposing protected implementation details.

**Live report:** https://atlas-ascend.github.io/-PROMETHEUS-AIS-Competition/#case-study

## Evidence objects and receipt separation

**E-01 · Server topology capture**
The PROMETHEUS Forge server separates onboarding, live case-study publication, proof indexing, mission control, adversarial work, ProofGrid, bridge control, telemetry, and incident/rollback concerns.

**E-02 · ServerForge application publication**
Observed campaign: `HYDRA-SERVERFORGE-20260719T023826Z`
Observed deployment receipt:
```text
26ccd3be5d15b170e24e54855db50f747
ebf3c2fbfccac3622fb629b07665ffc
```
This is the **ServerForge deployment receipt** observed in Discord. It is deliberately kept separate from the repository’s **ProofGrid mission receipt**, which verifies the executable competition mission, tests, artifacts, hashes, Genome reuse, and promotion state.

**E-03 · Repository evidence**
Executable behavior, tests, receipt verification, artifact hashes, Capability Genome data, CI, and public documentation provide the primary technical inspection surface.

## Verified findings and disclosure boundary

**Verified competition state**
```text
5 / 5 tests passed
0 tests failed
8 / 8 evidence artifacts verified
ProofGrid receipt hash verified
Capability Genome preserved and reused
1 failed action → 0 failed actions
Build Truth promotion authorized
```

**Ghost Atlas credibility model**
Provenance · Object separation · Corroboration · Fail-closed claims · Reproducibility · Disclosure control

**Public:** system responsibilities, observable transitions, campaign identifiers already visible in evidence, bounded failure/repair behavior, public proof totals, receipt lineage, and high-level deployment topology.

**Withheld:** private prompts, credentials, webhooks, server IDs, machine paths, private repositories, proprietary routing, recursive mechanisms, autonomous recovery internals, patent-sensitive algorithms, and unrelated estate architecture.

> Completion is not a statement. It is a state authorized by evidence.
proof-index:
# PROMETHEUS Competition Proof Index
**Field report:** `GAI-FR-20260721-PROM-SF-001`

**Operator Console and case study**
https://atlas-ascend.github.io/-PROMETHEUS-AIS-Competition/#case-study

**Public competition repository**
https://github.com/Atlas-Ascend/-PROMETHEUS-AIS-Competition

**Machine-readable ProofGrid receipt**
https://atlas-ascend.github.io/-PROMETHEUS-AIS-Competition/proofgrid/FINAL_RECEIPT.json

**Independent verification commands**
```text
python -m prometheus.cli receipt verify artifacts/competition-demo/FINAL_RECEIPT.json
python -m prometheus.cli contest evidence-verify
python -m prometheus.cli contest claims-verify
python -m prometheus.cli contest submission-check
```

## Evidence-object map

`SERVERFORGE DEPLOYMENT RECEIPT`
Records the Discord-observed ServerForge promotion publication for campaign `HYDRA-SERVERFORGE-20260719T023826Z`.

`PROOFGRID MISSION RECEIPT`
Records the executable competition mission, five passing tests, eight verified evidence artifacts, receipt integrity, Capability Genome reuse, and authorized promotion.

`PUBLIC OPERATOR CONSOLE`
Presents a deterministic visualization of the verified snapshot and remains inspectable when EDEN is offline.

`EDEN LOCAL EXECUTION BODY`
The Windows-based repository, runtime, command environment, and development tooling where the real engineering work was performed. Private machine state is not published.
